sampled.snippet: Exhaustive case-insensitive ripgrep across content.txt and every sections/*.txt for: RadImageNet, ResNet50, ResNet 50, ResNet-50, '1.4 million', '1,400,000', '670 000', '670,000', RINFD, supervised, Mei. Zero hits across all phrasings. The vandersluijs2023 paper is about Med-VAE neural compression of chest X-ray and full-field digital mammograms, not RadImageNet or any MRI/ResNet50 work.

observed.at_claimed_location: Re-ran `rg -i 'RadImageNet|ResNet|RINFD|1.4 million|670' /pdfs/vandersluijs2023/content.txt` -> exit code 1 (zero matches). Re-ran the same regex across /pdfs/vandersluijs2023/sections/ -> zero matches for the substantive terms RadImageNet, ResNet, RINFD, '1.4 million', or '670'. Confirmed via `meta.json` abstract: 'Medical images (e.g. X-rays) are often acquired at high resolutions... we (1) use over one million medical images to train a domain-specific neural compressor and (2) develop a comprehensive evaluation suite for measuring compressed image quality.' Paper subject is a domain-specific neural compressor (VAE) for X-ray / mammography, with no RadImageNet, ResNet50, MRI, or RINFD content.
observed.note: Absence of all substantive terms (RadImageNet, ResNet, ResNet50, ResNet-50, RINFD, '1.4 million', '670', Mei) is confirmed across content.txt and sections/*.txt. The sampled snippet's broader claim of 'Zero hits across all phrasings' has one minor caveat: the term 'supervised' appears once in sections/'Evaluation Suite.txt' in the phrase 'supervised linear probing (Zhang et al., 2016)' as part of HRNet downstream classification on chest X-rays / mammograms — this is unrelated to the RadImageNet supervised-classifier training regime being claimed and does not undermine the negative-evidence verdict. All claim-specific terms (RadImageNet/ResNet/RINFD/1.4M/670/Mei) remain absent. Paper subject (Med-VAE neural compression of chest X-ray + full-field digital mammograms) is unrelated to the RadImageNet/ResNet50/MRI claim. The CITED_OUT_OF_CONTEXT verdict for C067.a is fully supported.
